overview.short: Sumargad is a less-visited fort in the Ratnagiri district.
overview.description: Sumargad is a less-visited fort in the Ratnagiri district. This fort lies east of the Khed. It is about 19 kilometres (12 mi) from Khed City. There are no human settlements around the fort.

topPlaces.name: Wadi-jaitapur
topPlaces.description: Wadi-jaitapur is a small village in Ratnagiri district, Maharashtra state in Western India. The 2011 census of India recorded a total of 289 residents in the village.

topPlaces.name: Ghogare
topPlaces.description: Ghogare is a small village in Ratnagiri district, Maharashtra state in Western India. The 2011 Census of India recorded a total of 431 residents in the village.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Suamargad?
faq.a: The best time to visit Suamargad is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

The nearest airport is Pune Airport (96 km) and the nearest railway station is Pune Junction (89 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Suamargad?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Suamargad at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Suamargad?
faq.a: Stays in Suamargad start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Suamargad?
faq.a: The top attractions are Wadi-jaitapur, Ghogare, Shiravali, Bholavali.
